options.text: beyond one's capability to understand something

questions.question_text_full:
Select the most appropriate indirect form of the given sentence.
The thief said to the judge, “Pardon me.”

options.text: The thief begged the judge to pardon him.
The thief requested the judge for pardon me.
The thief said to the judge to pardon.
The thief urged to pardon to the judge.

questions.correct_option_text: The thief begged the judge to pardon him.

questions.question_text_full:
Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order.
A. Day in and day out he pondered to find out a foolproof plan to capture Robin Hood.
B. The Sheriff did not want to give up the idea of capturing Robin Hood.
C. He shut himself in his chamber devising one plan after another.
D. He did not discuss his plans with anyone because he trusted nobody.

Select the correct active form of the given sentence.
The ticket will be sent to you by the airlines on e-mail.

options.text: The airlines had sent you the ticket by e-mail.
The airlines will have sent you the ticket by e-mail.
The airlines will send you the ticket by e-mail.
The airlines will be sending you the ticket by e-mail.

questions.correct_option_text: The airlines will send you the ticket by e-mail.
